name: Orange Brandy Cordial
recipeInstructions:
1. Thinly peel the oranges using a vegetable peeler, taking care not to include any pith.
2. Cut the orange strips into 1/4-inch segments.
3. Add the orange peels and clove to a 1-quart mason jar.
4. Pour brandy over the fruit to fill the jar; seal tightly.
5. Allow the mixture to stand in a cool, dark place for 10 days.
6. Strain the liquid through a fine sieve into a 4-cup glass measuring cup; discard solids.
7. Rinse the sieve and mason jar thoroughly.
8. Fit the sieve with a damp coffee filter and strain the flavored brandy back into the jar.
9. In a small saucepan, heat sugar and water to boiling for about 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
10. Remove from heat; let cool for 5 minutes.
11. Add the sugar syrup and glycerin to the muddled brandy in the jar.
12. Seal the jar and gently shake to combine.
13. If desired, transfer the cordial to a decanter. The Orange Brandy Cordial will be ready to drink within 2-3 days and can be stored for up to 2-3 months.
